nativeRange.habitat: Rocky and coral reef bottoms in clear water, typically at depths of 4–70 m; reef-associated and often found in groups on deeper reefs

diet.notes: Generally accepts meaty foods such as finely chopped fresh or frozen marine fish, squid, mysis shrimp, and enriched brine shrimp. In the wild, has been observed following goatfishes as they probe sand for invertebrates, suggesting opportunistic feeding on disturbed prey.

imageCandidates.notes: FishBase professional photo, Jamaica, photographer M.A. Steele. Full URL constructed from thumbnail path tn_Setab_u4.jpg on the species summary page (ID 3349).
FishBase professional photo, Saint Vincent and Grenadines, photographer L. Pialek.

summary: The Tobacco Basslet is a vividly patterned fish from Western Atlantic and Caribbean reefs, recognized by its bold orange-banded coloration. Generally considered suitable for reef aquariums, though caution is advised around very small fish and ornamental crustaceans.
careNotes: The Tobacco Basslet (Serranus tabacarius) is a synchronous hermaphrodite, meaning every individual in the genus Serranus is capable of functioning as either male or female at the same time. Multiple specimens can generally be kept together if all are introduced to the aquarium simultaneously, which tends to reduce dominance disputes; adding a new specimen to a tank with an established individual is more likely to cause conflict. Larger adults have been known to threaten small gobies, blennies, damselfish, and ornamental crustaceans, so tankmate selection should take the species' adult size — up to around 20 cm — into account. Although the Tobacco Basslet is reef-associated in the wild, individuals in captivity may investigate or nip at very small ornamental invertebrates, and reef compatibility is generally rated with caution rather than fully safe.
breedingNotes: The Tobacco Basslet is a synchronous hermaphrodite, and captive breeding has not been documented in hobbyist aquariums.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (FishBase max 22 cm TL vs LiveAquaria 7 in / ~18 cm), temperature range (FishBase 24.8–28°C vs LiveAquaria 72–78°F / 22–26°C), native distribution, taxonomy, and diet. Size and temperature values averaged across both sources. FishBase species ID 3349.

fish.breedingNotes: Synchronous hermaphrodite; captive breeding not documented in hobbyist settings.
